items.title: 36-318 Introduction to Causal Inference
items.source_text: Topics include propensity score methods, matching estimators, doubly robust estimation, instrumental variables, and regression discontinuity designs. The course also covers difference-in-differences, causal graph models (DAGs), and treatment effect heterogeneity in observational data settings.

items.title: 36-462 Statistical Machine Learning
items.source_text: The course covers clustering, dimensionality reduction, and supervised learning methods including regression with regularization, classification, decision trees, random forests, and boosting. Emphasis on model selection via cross-validation and understanding the statistical properties of machine learning algorithms.

items.title: 19-629 Climate Change Science and Solutions
items.source_text: Provides an overview of climate science alongside projected climate consequences, emission reduction technologies, and public policy adaptation strategies. Covers greenhouse gas emissions accounting, energy transition policy, carbon pricing mechanisms, and climate risk assessment for engineering and policy applications.

items.title: 19-701 Introduction to the Theory and Practice of Policy Analysis
items.source_text: Examines analytical techniques for policy analysis including valuation of intangibles, uncertainty in policy analysis, selected topics in risk analysis, and behavioral decision theory. Covers cost-benefit analysis, multi-criteria evaluation frameworks, and stakeholder analysis for evidence-based policy decisions.

items.title: 94-802 Geographic Information Systems
items.source_text: A GIS provides storage, retrieval, and visualization of geographically referenced data and unique analytical tools for investigating spatial relationships, patterns, and processes. Topics include proximity analysis, risk surface modeling, site suitability analysis, spatial data mining, raster GIS, and network analysis using ArcGIS Pro and ArcGIS Online.

items.title: 90-722 Management Science I: Optimization and Decision Modeling
items.source_text: Core technique is optimization applied to public policy decision-making. Topics include linear programming, decision analysis, sensitivity analysis, multi-objective optimization, network flow problems, and integer programming. Students develop optimization models to evaluate policy alternatives and resource allocation decisions.
